title: Bonneville Speedway - Wikipedia
content:
### Coordinates
| Format | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| Degrees, Minutes, Seconds | 40°45′45″N 113°53′44″W |
| Decimal | 40.76250°N 113.89556°W |

### Course Lengths
| Course Type | Length (Imperial) | Length (Metric) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Traditional Speed Week Courses | 9 miles | 14 km |
| Original Straightaway (Speed Trials) | 10 miles | 16 km |
| Original Oval or Circular Track | 10 to 12 miles | 16 to 19 km |
| Reduced Available Racing Surface (2014–2015) | 2.5 miles | 4.0 km |
| Mud-covered track section (2014–2015) | Approximately 6 mi | 9.7 km |

### Track Layout and Timed Segment Details
*   **Measured Mile Course Length:** The page does not state an exact total length for a specific course named the "measured mile course." It provides the following details regarding timed mile segments:
    *   **Large Public Meets (e.g., Speed Week):** Utilize as many as four tracks with several timed miles. Timed sections usually start with the second mile and run to the fifth mile.
    *   **Smaller Meets (World Record Attempts):** Utilize a single track with one timed mile and one timed kilometer located in the middle of the track.
*   **Track Markers:**
    *   Historically: A broad black line down the center.
    *   Later: Black lines down either side (discontinued after August 2009).
    *   Current: Flags and cones.
    *   Additional markers and cones indicate the end of the track and timing equipment positions.

### Course Length Verification
*   **Surveying:** Surveyors are brought in to survey the timing trap distances.
*   **Timing Equipment:** Timing equipment positions are indicated by marks and cones.
*   **Preparation Responsibility:** Originally marked by the Utah Department of Transportation; since at least the 1990s, event organizers are responsible for identifying the area, grading the tracks, and bringing in surveyors.
